tests.source_program:
define i1 @fp16_half_isnan(half %a) {
  %i16 = bitcast half %a to i16
  %and1 = and i16 %i16, 31744
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i16 %and1, 31744
  %and2 = and i16 %i16, 1023
  %cmp2 = icmp ne i16 %and2, 0
  %res = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %res
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary explicitly mentions float and double types but does not mention half precision (fp16). This tests the generalization to half precision floating point which is IEEE-like but not covered in the summary text.

alive2.src:
define i1 @fp16_half_isnan(half %a) {
  %i16 = bitcast half %a to i16
  %and1 = and i16 %i16, 31744
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i16 %and1, 31744
  %and2 = and i16 %i16, 1023
  %cmp2 = icmp ne i16 %and2, 0
  %res = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %res
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @isnotnan_idiom_or_pattern(double %x) {
  %bits = bitcast double %x to i64
  %mask1 = and i64 %bits, 9218868437227405312
  %cond1 = icmp ne i64 %mask1, 9218868437227405312
  %mask2 = and i64 %bits, 4503599627370495
  %cond2 = icmp eq i64 %mask2, 0
  %ret = or i1 %cond1, %cond2
  ret i1 %ret
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions isnotnan idioms but all existing tests show isnan (FCMP_UNO) patterns with AND. This tests the complementary isnotnan pattern using OR with negated conditions that folds to FCMP_ORD.

  %mask2 = and i64 %bits, 4503599627370495
  %cond2 = icmp eq i64 %mask2, 0
  %ret = or i1 %cond1, %cond2
  ret i1 %ret
}

tests.source_program:
define double @isnan_in_select_condition(double %x, double %v1, double %v2) {
  %bits = bitcast double %x to i64
  %mask1 = and i64 %bits, 9218868437227405312
  %cond1 = icmp eq i64 %mask1, 9218868437227405312
  %mask2 = and i64 %bits, 4503599627370495
  %cond2 = icmp ne i64 %mask2, 0
  %isnan = and i1 %cond1, %cond2
  %result = select i1 %isnan, double %v1, double %v2
  ret double %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on direct return of the isnan check result. This tests the pattern where the isnan idiom result is used as a condition in a select instruction, representing a different usage context not explicitly covered.

alive2.src:
define double @isnan_in_select_condition(double %x, double %v1, double %v2) {
  %bits = bitcast double %x to i64
  %mask1 = and i64 %bits, 9218868437227405312
  %cond1 = icmp eq i64 %mask1, 9218868437227405312
  %mask2 = and i64 %bits, 4503599627370495
  %cond2 = icmp ne i64 %mask2, 0
  %isnan = and i1 %cond1, %cond2
  %result = select i1 %isnan, double %v1, double %v2
  ret double %result
}

  %result = select i1 %isnan, double %v1, double %v2
  ret double %result
}
